objective: Deliver a company-private ultra-thin engineer Web client backed by the official Codex Python SDK and Codex agent runtime, preserve the existing Haystack/DuckDB site and synthetic HVAC corpus as comparison assets, and verify the new path with governed read-only tools, reproducible tests, browser acceptance, independent review and deployment handoff.

contract: Codex SDK or Codex App Server is the primary intelligence backend behind one lightweight engineer-facing Web client
The Web client has one free-chat entry, a few high-frequency workflow buttons, readable Markdown, bounded tables/charts and original-filename citations
OpenCode is a replaceable alternative only if current commercial or provider evidence rejects Codex; it is not selected merely because it is MIT licensed
Formal deployment uses a company API, Azure OpenAI, or approved Business/Enterprise credentials and never shares a personal login or API key
The current synthetic HVAC corpus and old Web backend remain frozen comparison assets
The Obsidian-style graph is secondary evidence navigation: compact local graph after evidence use, full project graph only on demand

Acceptance includes harder multi-part questions, explicit honest benchmark boundaries, independent review, browser acceptance, documentation, commit and push; an architecture report alone is not completion
latest_direct_correction.commercial_evidence: OpenAI Services Agreement effective 2026-01-01 permits API integration into Customer Applications and access by End Users; account sharing, account-access resale and API-key transfer remain prohibited. openai/codex is Apache-2.0.

input: one natural-language project question or a named high-frequency workflow
an isolated project copy containing approved documents and read-only datasets
process: the official Codex Python SDK starts the pinned Codex App Server in an ephemeral session
Codex performs iterative evidence gathering inside an isolated copied workspace
a required official MCP server exposes typed bounded read-only HVAC operations and one guarded flat SELECT escape hatch
structured output is validated and rendered by the existing Chat UI
the compact evidence graph appears only after a query uses sources and expands to the full project map only when requested
output: optional bounded tables and line/bar charts backed by governed results
bounded tool activity without private chain-of-thought
exact human-readable source filenames and validated excerpts
measured comparison evidence without fabricated or silently degraded results

selection.runtime: official openai-codex Python SDK 0.144.4 controlling its pinned bundled Codex App Server; the measured CLI JSONL transport remains an explicit comparison adapter only
selection.sdk_status: The official Python SDK 0.144.4 is published as beta. It supports async threads, streaming, structured output and explicit sandbox selection. The project pins it exactly, hash-locks the install, and never silently falls back to CLI.
selection.provider: custom company-approved OpenAI-compatible Responses provider with supports_websockets=false
selection.data_tool: official MCP Python SDK 1.28.1 plus DuckDB 1.5.4; nine bounded read-only knowledge/HVAC tools, typed operations preferred, guarded single flat SELECT only as an escape hatch; arbitrary Shell is rejected
selection.graph: reuse vendored Cytoscape 3.34.0 for an Obsidian-style local/full evidence graph; no second renderer or graph database added
selection.policy: Synthetic public-data SDK contract evaluation may run only in its explicit test profile. Company-data deployment remains fail-closed until the execution boundary denies application and unrelated host reads; WSL2 Landlock remains the validated production-isolation candidate.
selection.ui: reuse the current single-Chat page; no fifth visual architecture

measured_evidence.claim: The company Responses endpoint works with Codex when configured as a custom provider with supports_websockets=false.
measured_evidence.result: turn.completed

measured_evidence.claim: The earlier architecture probe combined governed data-quality/COP operations with project documents where the old backend refused the equivalent MX01 result.
measured_evidence.result: Codex probe completed under the weaker sandbox; old backend hard_gate_pass=false and refused=true. Architecture evidence only.

measured_evidence.claim: The official Python SDK adapter starts the real bundled Codex App Server, preserves a controlled environment and parses actual SDK event types.
measured_evidence.result: 44 passed including local mock Responses integration, strict-config failure, document-tool provenance adversaries, double-negative question-number grounding rejection and raw-API citation-metadata redaction

measured_evidence.claim: The official MCP STDIO lifecycle exposes all nine governed knowledge/HVAC tools with constrained schemas and executes a typed configuration-change query.
measured_evidence.result: tools discovered; configuration change returned approved 12.0 to 10.0 evidence

measured_evidence.claim: Focused runtime, direction and Web regressions remain green after SDK, MCP, structured presentation and graph integration.
measured_evidence.result: 80 runtime/direction/Web tests passed; the frozen complex-question/evaluator contract added 10 tests, for 90 focused tests total

measured_evidence.claim: The engineer-facing evidence graph is unobtrusive and usable.
measured_evidence.result: 10 passed, 1 intentionally gated real-model case skipped; compact local graph, expand/restore, exact cited-dataset highlighting and fixed composer verified

measured_evidence.claim: The frozen old-backend hard subset establishes a reproducible baseline.
measured_evidence.evidence: existing evaluation result and human-review artifacts
measured_evidence.result: hard gate 1/9; human 0 pass, 3 partial, 6 fail; this is not an SDK score

measured_evidence.claim: The authorized elevated Windows setup completed, but the sandbox account could still open application/root-repository files outside the copied workspace.
measured_evidence.evidence: local OS probes on 2026-07-18 after codex sandbox setup --elevated --current-user
measured_evidence.result: setup exit=0; private DuckDB with explicit ACL exit=73; root repository read exit=0; Windows production startup remains blocked

measured_evidence.claim: The existing WSL2 environment enforces the intended allow-only read boundary with the official Codex Linux runtime.
measured_evidence.evidence: local WSL2 Landlock probe using a custom Permission Profile
measured_evidence.result: workspace allowed exit=0; outside private file exit=1; mounted Windows root repository exit=1

measured_evidence.claim: Independent review findings for document/data evidence binding, question-number grounding, Shell containment, strict configuration, citation-metadata redaction, exact graph highlighting and MCP schema usability were remediated with adversarial tests.
measured_evidence.result: final independent rereview Ready: Yes with no Critical or Important finding; one minor descendant-timeout-cleanup risk remains documented

measured_evidence.claim: The bounded Codex SDK and Obsidian-style evidence-graph delivery was committed and pushed without force.
measured_evidence.result: origin/codex/agentic-rag-bakeoff advanced from 74ad158 to cfa8dc8

measured_evidence.claim: Current official OpenCode evidence supports the replaceable SDK, local MCP and deny-by-default permissions adapter.
measured_evidence.result: OpenCode v1.18.3 is MIT and active; no parity claim is made from license or popularity

measured_evidence.claim: Existing simple and first-complex OpenCode payloads replay through the strict adapter before new model calls.
measured_evidence.result: simple 4 tools/7 citations grounded; first-complex 6 tools/10 citations grounded with four-table output cap

measured_evidence.claim: The OpenCode worker and grounding/display seams were fixed with TDD after reproducible failures.
measured_evidence.evidence: tests/test_opencode_runtime.py, tests/test_codex_runtime.py, tests/test_direction_agent.py and working-tree OpenCode files
measured_evidence.result: v1 SDK path/query/body mismatch, wide-table grounding loss, aggregate presentation overflow and duplicate pytz dependency are covered by regression tests

measured_evidence.claim: Two predeclared same-model synthetic OpenCode cases completed with grounded evidence and safety behavior.
measured_evidence.result: CC01 194.939s/3 tools/6 citations; CC02 151.046s/3 tools/6 citations; CC02 completed read-only work and refused write/control

measured_evidence.claim: A live Codex quality control was not run because the native Windows isolation preflight is absent/failed closed.
measured_evidence.evidence: docs/CODEX_RUNTIME_CHECKPOINT.md and the current preflight command output
measured_evidence.result: No Codex parity or winner is claimed; WSL2/Landlock remains the production isolation follow-up

measured_evidence.claim: Independent review found and closed the private-Agent egress, discarded-citation grounding and OpenCode descendant-cleanup defects.
measured_evidence.evidence: tests/test_codex_runtime.py, tests/test_opencode_runtime.py, tests/test_web_app.py and the independent rereview
measured_evidence.result: Legacy remote APIs return 409 in fixed Agent modes; discarded citation metadata cannot ground numbers; Windows taskkill nonzero/timeout/OSError fail closed; rereview Ready: Yes

measured_evidence.claim: The comparison boundary and stale CC02 gold correction are machine-readable without rewriting the frozen contract.
measured_evidence.evidence: evaluation/test_opencode_codex_comparison_contract.py and the CC01/CC02 OpenCode/Codex result artifacts
measured_evidence.result: CC02 semantic facts pass with contract correction; both Codex controls record blocked_by_isolation, model_call_started=false and comparison_eligible=false

measured_evidence.claim: Final focused and full verification passed after all review fixes and lock regeneration.
measured_evidence.result: Ruff check/format passed; 449 tests passed and 14 intentional tests skipped after the CI lock fix; release guard passed

measured_evidence.claim: All hash locks preserve MCP's pywin32 dependency as Windows-only so Ubuntu CI can install the shared dependency graph.
measured_evidence.evidence: pyproject.toml, requirements.lock, requirements.runtime.lock, requirements.documents.lock, requirements.documents-ci.lock and tests/test_packaging.py
measured_evidence.result: All four locks contain pywin32==312 with sys_platform == win32; the regression and document-lock parity tests pass

safety_boundaries: No company data, credentials, endpoint values, runtime indexes or model sessions enter Git.
The browser never receives an API key.
Each request receives a fresh ephemeral project copy; hidden benchmark truth and repository internals are excluded.
DuckDB remains outside command read permissions and is reachable only through the required MCP server.
Typed MCP tools are preferred; the SQL tool permits one bounded flat read-only SELECT and denies file/system access, writes, star projection, CTEs and subqueries.
Every Shell, file-change and Web-search event is rejected; document inspection is available only through search_project_knowledge.

Physical document citations must come from search_project_knowledge, virtual data citations must be authorized by the exact MCP result, and model-authored numeric claims/tables/charts must match returned tool or cited evidence.
All public answer, table and chart strings receive server-side internal-path redaction before the API response.
A custom Permission Profile grants only minimal runtime reads, one copied document workspace, temporary writes, and no command network access.
Native Windows deployment fails closed while unrelated host reads remain possible.
Web search and direct network access are disabled inside project-analysis turns.
OnionQuant Cron, context_state, Inbox, Outbox and runtime state remain untouched.
Existing unrelated untracked evaluation results remain untouched.

slices.title: Current Web/GitHub research, commercial verification and runtime feasibility
TDD SDK transport, structured output and governed MCP implementation
Thin Chat UI, named workflows and Obsidian-style evidence graph
Frozen comparison contract and browser acceptance
slices.note: Old-backend baseline and SDK contract are recorded separately. A full native-Windows real-model SDK quality score is deliberately not claimed because the host-read isolation gate fails.

slices.title: Deployment handoff, independent review, final verification, commit and push
OpenCode SDK replay, same-model comparison and adapter hardening
acceptance: existing simple and first-complex payloads replayed
two predeclared complex cases measured with latency/tool/grounding/citation/safety evidence
Codex control status recorded without bypassing native-Windows isolation
focused/full verification and independent review passed; public-safe commit and non-force push follow this checkpoint

current_blocker: The public-safe OpenCode adapter, two OpenCode measurements, review fixes and release gates are complete. A live Codex cross-backend quality control remains blocked by the native-Windows host-read isolation gate; machine-readable artifacts record that no Codex model call started, and no parity or winner claim is made.
